The outside view is extremely unfavorable: the provided empirical base rate for FIDE Elo +1% questions is 0.007, and for a 2813-2816 player this means needing roughly a +29 point jump to about 2842-2845. That is enormous at world-#2 level. The Wikipedia page we fetched is clearly on the monthly FIDE list cadence ('ranked on 1 November 2025'), so for 2025-11-16 the page is very likely still showing the November list, making YES only a floor-level chance. One targeted search did show Nakamura was unusually active in classical events late in 2025 because of the Candidates rating/40-game requirement, which gives him more opportunities by December and especially February. But those extra events included low-rated opens where each win yields only tiny Elo gains while any draw/loss is costly, so the chance of reaching a full +1% remains very low. Hence I keep all three probabilities near the lower bound, rising modestly with horizon.

Wikipedia's own list still showed the Gonorrhea vaccine field blank in the 2025-10-01 revision, and the prompt says the most recent known status as of 2025-10-30 was No. The additional context I found points the same way: no approved gonorrhea vaccine exists; the leading named candidate was still only in Phase II; and a 2024 review said work was early-stage with only one ongoing human trial. The main upside risk is definitional: an existing meningococcal B vaccine appears to offer moderate cross-protection against gonorrhea, so Wikipedia could someday decide that counts. But a change from No to Yes within days or a few months still looks unlikely. I therefore put the shortest horizon at the floor and raise the later dates only slightly.

This looks very unlikely across all three dates. The outside view is that a neglected infectious disease with no currently listed vaccine almost never flips to 'vaccine exists' within 1 week to 3 months. The inside view is also negative: the question background says the most recent known Wikipedia status on 2025-10-30 was No, and the best search evidence indicates there has been no real vaccine-development push for relapsing fever specifically. A 2019 review says there has been no interest in developing vaccines against relapsing fever borreliae, and more recent review material indicates the science is still early and complicated by multiple Borrelia species. So the probability of Wikipedia showing a vaccine by 2025-11-16 is near the floor. I raise the later dates slightly only because longer horizons allow a bit more chance of an editorial change or obscure preclinical development being recognized, but the odds remain low.

The Wikipedia section shows her at 2569 on the 1 Nov 2025 women’s list, so if her 2025-11-09 value is around that level, she likely needs about 2595 to resolve YES. That is a very large move for an elite player. For 2025-11-16, the key point is that FIDE ratings are monthly, so the Wikipedia value one week later is very likely unchanged from 2025-11-09; I therefore sit at the floor. For 2025-12-09, only one monthly list update can really matter, and I found no clear evidence of a substantial November classical schedule that would make a +26 surge plausible. By 2026-02-07 there are several rating cycles and more opportunity, so the chance is higher, but still low because +26 from an already elite base is uncommon.

Trachoma is caused by Chlamydia trachomatis, and the available pre-cutoff evidence points to an active but still early vaccine pipeline rather than a developed vaccine. Recent sources say there was no vaccine / no licensed vaccine, the leading candidate had only phase-1 data and was being discussed for phase 2 or human-challenge work, and the Trachoma Wikipedia page’s prevention section did not mention any vaccine. Since the latest known Wikipedia status before cutoff was already No, a flip to Yes within 1 week, 1 month, or even 3 months looks very unlikely; I raise the probability only slightly with horizon because more time allows a bit more chance of a rapid breakthrough and Wikipedia update.

Base rate is 0.68, but this is a stronger-than-average hold case. On the latest available Wikipedia/FIDE list before the cutoff, Carlsen is already #1, so the question is effectively whether he stays #1. The 2025-11-16 date is only a week after the cutoff and still within the November ratings cycle, so it is very likely Wikipedia will still show Carlsen at #1. For 2025-12-09, the main risk is the December list, but Carlsen was recently inactive, and reporting indicates Carlsen, Nakamura, and Caruana were absent from the FIDE World Cup / other immediate classical opportunities, which limits near-term rating volatility. By 2026-02-07, there are more paths to a NO: two more monthly updates, possible January classical events, and more time for Nakamura or another elite player to put together a strong enough run to overtake. Still, Carlsen had remained world #1 continuously since 2011 as of early November 2025, so YES remains more likely than not even at the longest horizon.

The 2025-11-09 benchmark is effectively about 2742, so a YES requires roughly 2769.4+ (practically 2770). The key point is that this is not really a 'brand-new vaccine invented in the next few weeks' question. There is evidence that at least one commercial veterinary vaccine for pasteurellosis/fowl cholera already exists, so a future Wikipedia flip to 'Yes' could happen without new science. However, the resolution source was still reportedly 'No' on 2025-10-30, which means Wikipedia either has not been updated or may be using a narrower interpretation (for example, not counting species-specific veterinary vaccines as enough for the generic disease row). That makes a near-term flip possible but still unlikely. I therefore assign a low chance over 1 week, a somewhat higher chance over 1 month, and a higher-but-still-minority chance by early February as there is more time for editors to notice and revise the entry.

Wei Yi is shown at 2753 on the Wikipedia FIDE rankings page’s 1 Nov 2025 list, and the prompt says the most recent known value on 2025-10-30 was 2754. So the relevant +1% threshold is about 2781, roughly 28 Elo higher than his early-November level. That is a very large jump for an established top-20 player. For 2025-11-16, the key point is that the Wikipedia page appears to reflect monthly FIDE lists, so one week later it is very likely still effectively the same November list. I therefore put the near-term date at the floor and only modestly higher for the later dates.

The key fact is that as of the cutoff, Wikipedia’s relevant entry was still effectively NO, and outside sources before 2025-11-09 consistently said there was no licensed norovirus vaccine anywhere. There is one important positive: Moderna’s mRNA-1403 had reached Phase 3, so this is not a purely speculative area. But the near-term negatives are much stronger: the Phase 3 program had faced an FDA clinical hold after a Guillain-Barré report, and even by Nov. 6 the readout timing was still described as dependent on case accruals. That makes a Wikipedia change within one week or one month extremely unlikely, and still unlikely by early February. I raise the probability slightly with horizon because more time allows for topline data and a possible Wikipedia update, but the overall odds remain low.

BG’s threshold is effectively the 2025-11-07 close of about 93.49, since 2025-11-09 was a Sunday. My primary anchor is the price-history tool, which gave a combined estimate of 0.42 for exceeding that level by 2025-11-16. Recent momentum into the cutoff was mildly negative, though the broader 3-month linear trend was still slightly positive. Company-specific news was mixed: Bunge had lowered 2025 EPS guidance after closing the Viterra deal, and Reuters highlighted weak grain prices, soft processing margins, and policy/trade uncertainty; but it also beat Q3 expectations, reaffirmed guidance, and cited favorable developing conditions plus merger-related strategic benefits. Net: slightly sub-50 for the nearest date, then probabilities drift back toward 0.5 as horizon increases and short-term technical signals matter less.

Reference class: short-horizon single-stock price-above-current-close questions. I start from the empirical base rate of 0.576, but for this question I anchor more on the stock-specific price history because weekly/monthly stock moves are noisy and usually near 50/50. Since 2025-11-09 is a non-trading day, the effective comparison price is the 2025-11-07 close of 67.52. The Yahoo-price tool gives a combined 0.59 estimate for being above 67.52 by 2025-11-16, but I discount that somewhat because the seasonal component is based on only one prior year and the 3-month trend into the cutoff was strongly negative. Context from the latest earnings/guidance is mixed: operating results were solid, but the market still sold the stock off sharply after the report, suggesting elevated expectations and lingering bearish sentiment. Net: modest edge to a rebound above this depressed threshold in the near term, fading toward a coin flip at longer horizons.
